name: Thumbnail
description: Renders a rasterized SVG thumbnail via image-extractor — same tab name, same underlying extractor as thumbnail/renderer.json, still a separate manifest because file_type "svg" needs to live somewhere distinct from that one's own raster-format list. file_mode "any" (not "binary") is the real reason this needs its own manifest now, though: SVG is genuinely both real text content and a rasterizable image at once (isTextual is now content-sniff-only — see file-handler-detect.js — so a .svg file always lands as fileMode 'text', consistently, regardless of whether file-type's signature-based MIME detection happens to recognise it). "any" lets this match regardless of that, so both the raw-text tab and this thumbnail are always offered together for every SVG. No binary_category — deliberately forbidden by the schema for "any" renderers, since fileMode isn't consulted at all here to begin with.
